title: Scope and Core Invariants
context: This document fills the C5 Runtime Scheduling context with executable core invariants.
responsibilities: Decide which runtime can claim or execute a task.
Compare task required surfaces with runtime capability snapshots.
Define runtime lease pinning and heartbeat meaning.
non_responsibilities: Provider static capability contracts belong to riido-contracts C3.
Provider process execution, provider session schema, retention, and adapters belong to C4 Provider Runtime.
Workdir creation belongs to the daemon migration workdir slice, and lock primitives belong to C9 Locking.
Client task-thread read models and presentation copy belong to control-plane and client.

invariants.name: capability-only-dispatch
invariants.summary: Schedulers branch on capability and task requirement facts, not provider binary version strings.

invariants.name: no-provider-specific-fsm
invariants.summary: Scheduling compares requirement/capability envelopes; execution state is owned by the C1/C2 task FSM.

invariants.name: spawn-after-eligibility
invariants.summary: Provider processes are spawned only after the C5 pre-submit eligibility gate accepts the task.

invariants.name: experimental-runtime-opt-in
invariants.summary: Experimental runtimes require explicit task or assignment opt-in.

invariants.name: lease-runtime-pin
invariants.summary: A runtime lease is pinned by RuntimeID plus CapabilityFingerprint; changed fingerprints make stale leases invalid.

invariants.name: file-queue-not-durable-scheduler
invariants.summary: The local file queue is an adapter source; durable scheduling semantics belong to DB/API sources.

invariants.name: task-db-production-source
invariants.summary: riido-task-db.v1 claims queued rows through guarded transitions and keeps provider-completed tasks in validation.

invariants.name: provider-session-outside-c5
invariants.summary: C5 preserves lease metadata but does not own the provider session table schema or native resume semantics.

invariants.name: client-read-model-outside-c5
invariants.summary: C5 preserves IDs for reporting but does not own task-thread read models, active_stream selection, or client copy.
